Complications and mortality of typhoid fever: A global systematic review and meta-analysis
What is the case fatality ratio of typhoid fever?
Global pooled CFR is 2.0% (95% CI 1.4-2.8%) for confirmed cases; higher in Africa (5.4%) than Asia (0.9%).[file:1]
What are the most common complications of typhoid fever?
Delirium (26.6%), anemia (21.4%), encephalopathy (3.6%), and TIP (1.3%). 26.3% of confirmed cases had complications.[file:1]
How does typhoid CFR differ by region?
Asia: 0.9%, Africa: 5.4%, with TIP CFR median 15.5% overall (20% Africa).[file:1]
Delays in care correlate with higher CFR (r=0.84 in Asia); hospital-based studies show higher rates than community.[file:1]
Emphasizes typhoid conjugate vaccine (TCV) to prevent complications and deaths, alongside sanitation improvements.[file:1]
Objectives: Updated estimates of the prevalence of complications and CFR... [truncated; use for RAG].
